agents.note:
Session 147 -- US-273 SHIPPED passes:true (Sprint 23 TIER 1-B story 3 of 4 unclaimed; records-hygiene closure of I-015 + I-016 + I-017). ZERO record edits required -- all THREE issue records ALREADY closed-in-fact at sprint groom time; current record state SATISFIES acceptance criterion #2 verbatim. Per CIO standing rule 'PM communication for missing stitching' + Sessions 144/145 deliberate-divergence pattern: shipped no-record-edit + filed inbox note for PM groom-knowledge feedback. FINDING per spec acceptance #1: I-015 line 6 'Resolved (2026-04-18, US-184 / Sprint 11, Ralph Session 46)' + dedicated ## Resolution at lines 40-86 (matches sub-clause 1: Resolved + closure note refs US-184 + Sprint 11). I-016 line 6 '**CLOSED BENIGN** (2026-04-20, Spool disposition via CIO drill -- see resolution below)' + dedicated ## Resolution (2026-04-20, Session 6 -- Spool) at lines 60-82 (matches sub-clause 2: Closed-Benign + closure note refs 2026-04-20 thermostat drill). I-017 line 6 '**Closed 2026-04-21 via US-218** -- canonicalization done.' + dedicated ## Closure (2026-04-21, US-218) at lines 12-62 (matches sub-clause 3: Resolved + closure note refs US-218 + Sprint 17). TWO artifacts shipped: offices/pm/inbox/2026-05-03-from-rex-us273-records-already-closed.md NEW (~95 lines) documenting (i) closure-in-fact-pre-existed finding, (ii) per-record acceptance-already-satisfied evidence with verbatim grep-output, (iii) why this happened (groom-knowledge gap: groundingRefs already pointed to closure-in-fact but acceptance/intent treated records as Open; both signals were available at groom time -- the inference step was missed), and (iv) recommendation that sprint_lint.py be extended to read filesToTouch issue records and detect already-closed Status at groom time -- US-274 territory or strict-superset extension; would catch BOTH directions of records-drift (the work-shipped-record-stayed-open direction US-272 had AND the records-already-closed direction US-273 has). offices/ralph/sprint.json US-273 passes:true + completedDate 2026-05-03 + ~3KB completionNotes documenting same. VERIFICATION: spec verification command #1 (grep '^| Status\|^Status:' on all 3 records) -- 3 matching lines all showing closed Status. Spec verification command #2 (python offices/pm/scripts/sprint_lint.py) -- 0 errors / 0 warnings across all 9 Sprint 23 stories (cleanest groom sustained through all 6 Sprint 23 stories shipped now). Full fast suite: pytest tests/ -m 'not slow' -q -- 3901 passed / 1 failed / 17 skipped / 19 deselected in 1095.37s (18:15). Baseline post-US-270 (Session 146) was 3901/1/17/19; delta +0 pass / 0 failure change -- correct because US-273 is a no-op records story (no test additions). The 1 residual failure is PRE-EXISTING on main and OUT OF SCOPE per Refusal Rule 3: tests/lint/test_no_hardcoded_addresses.py B-044 violation in scripts/seed_eclipse_vin.py:80,332,333 (already on main; flagged Sessions 139-146 close notes). Sprint-23-own-sprint failures: ZERO. STOP-CONDITION CHECK: stop-condition #1 'If any issue record cannot be cleanly closed because the referenced closing work is incomplete -- STOP, leave that record open + document in completionNotes' NOT triggered: all three closing references confirmed real (US-184 Sprint 11 / 2026-04-20 thermostat drill / US-218 Sprint 17). Both invariants satisfied: (i) no source code changes (records-only -- no record edits either since records already in target state); (ii) closure notes reference actual closing story/sprint (already in place pre-story). DELIBERATE DIVERGENCE: spec scope.filesToTouch listed I-015 + I-016 only (I-017 in filesToRead, not filesToTouch); intent + acceptance #2 + groundingRefs all explicitly named all THREE records. Discrepancy is a sprint-groom omission/typo; functionally clear that all 3 must be evaluated. Resolved by no-op (records already in target state) so no scope-fence violation occurred -- I-017 was read-only verified, not edited. Inbox note documents this as a third deliberate-divergence pattern to track alongside US-272 (rename-target-absent) and US-277 (/var/run ownership). PATTERN DISCOVERED (carryforward): closure-in-fact-pre-existed is a REVERSE records-drift pattern. US-272 had work-shipped-record-stayed-open shape (commit 57bdda6 closed TD-040 by deletion; record stayed open until US-272). US-273 has the OPPOSITE shape: records were ALREADY preemptively closed by their owning sprints (Sprint 11 US-184 for I-015; 2026-04-20 thermostat drill for I-016; Sprint 17 US-218 for I-017) so the groom premise was based on a stale read. Both directions of records-drift are detectable at groom time by parsing the Status field of each filesToTouch record. AI-001/US-274 territory or strict-superset extension. PATTERN REINFORCED: spec-acceptance pre-flight commands can return ZERO-DELTA results and that is itself a finding. Spec acceptance #1 'cat each I-015 + I-016 + I-017 record; confirm Status field currently shows Open or absent' -- the Status fields all showed CLOSED, not Open. That mismatch IS the load-bearing data point that triggered the no-op + inbox-note + passes:true path. Same shape as US-272 pre-flight (zero hits for seedVersionIsV0_18_0 was the load-bearing finding). Always interpret pre-flight results, don't just run them. PATTERN REINFORCED: spec-premise-was-false is a deliberate-divergence trigger, not a Refusal Rule 1 trigger. Refusal Rule 1 'ambiguity = blocker' applies when spec intent is unclear. Here intent was unambiguous (close 3 records); the premise was the issue (assumed Open; found Closed). The right move is shipping the functionally-correct version (no-op since acceptance already met) + filing inbox note for PM groom-knowledge feedback. Refusal Rule 1 BL-filing would have been over-cautious. Mirrors US-272 (rename-target-absent) and US-277 (/var/run ownership divergence) deliberate-divergence patterns -- a third instance of the pattern in this sprint. PATTERN OBSERVED: groundingRefs and acceptance can disagree at groom time. US-273 groundingRefs all explicitly pointed to closure-in-fact ('I-015 closed Sprint 11 via US-184'; 'I-016 CLOSED BENIGN 2026-04-20'; 'I-017 closed Sprint 17 via US-218') -- those ARE the canonical answer. Spec acceptance/intent treated the records as Open. The grooming step that needs strengthening: if groundingRefs say 'closed via X' AND records say 'closed via X', then there's no records-hygiene story to ship. Both signals were available at groom time. WORKING-TREE OBSERVATION (informational, not blocking): git status at session start showed 6 dirty files + 7 untracked from prior Sessions 142-144 (US-275 + US-276 + US-277) where their close-notes claim passes:true was shipped but the implementation files (src/pi/power/orchestrator.py + tests/pi/power/test_tick_internal_instrumentation.py + tests/pi/power/test_orchestrator_state_file.py + tests/deploy/test_drain_forensics_install.py + deploy/deploy-pi.sh + deploy/drain-forensics.service + offices/pm/inbox/2026-05-03-from-rex-us277-var-run-ownership-divergence.md) were apparently never committed. Same shape as US-262 Session 134/135 retroactive-ship-commit-096dade pattern. Sprint.json shows those 3 stories already passes:true. Per Refusal Rule 3 (Scope Fence) I touched ONLY US-273 scope: offices/pm/inbox/2026-05-03-from-rex-us273-records-already-closed.md (NEW), offices/ralph/sprint.json (US-273 update), offices/ralph/progress.txt (append), offices/ralph/ralph_agents.json (close-note + status:unassigned). The other dirty files left untouched for PM/CIO to resolve via retroactive-ship-commit pattern if needed. Phantom-path drift broke its 16-session pattern again: scope.filesToTouch listed real existing paths (no silent-substitute needed) -- 16 consecutive sessions broken now since Session 130. SPRINT 23 STATUS NOW: 6/9 SHIPPED (US-270 + US-272 + US-273 TIER 1-B + US-275 + US-276 + US-277 ALL TIER 1-A). Remaining unclaimed 3/9: TIER 1-B US-271 (S; TD-006 .env.example OLLAMA_BASE_URL doc; ~5-min trivial), US-278 (S; UPS HAT dropout characteristics doc; needs Spool's specs/grounded-knowledge.md inputs as grounding source); TIER 2 US-274 (M; sprint_lint file-existence check -- the durable AI-001 fix for phantom-path drift; biggest-impact-per-medium remaining; could be EXTENDED per US-273 inbox note recommendation to also detect already-closed Status on filesToTouch records, catching both records-drift directions). Recommended next-iteration pick: US-274 (M; the AI-001 fix; biggest sustained-impact return on the phantom-path-drift pattern AND extends to catch records-drift in both directions per US-272 + US-273 inbox notes; sole TIER 2 story; promotes the durable preventive measure). Alternatives: US-271 (S; .env.example doc-only addition; ~5-min trivial low-cognitive-load); US-278 (S; UPS HAT doc; needs Spool inputs).
